name: Grilled Tofu with Sesame Buckwheat Noodles &
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all marinade ingredients in a large bowl. Add the tofu pieces and cover with the marinade. Let marinate for 6 to 8 hours in the refrigerator or 3 hours at room temperature.
2. Prepare a charcoal grill.
3. Remove the tofu from the marinade, allowing excess to drip off. Cook over medium-hot coals, basting frequently with the marinade. Score the tofu on both sides using a cross-hatch pattern. Grill until golden brown, about 5 to 8 minutes per side. Remove from the grill and set aside.
3. For the sesame buckwheat noodles sauce: In a large bowl, combine green onions (minced), garlic (minced), miso (red or white), ginger (minced), jalapeno peppers (minced), sesame oil, peanut oil, seasoned rice wine vinegar, sherry vinegar, soy sauce, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Add toasted sesame seeds and cook the buckwheat noodles in salted boiling water until al dente. Drain, then add to the sauce mixture and toss. Let sit for about 20 minutes.
4. For the ginger mixed vegetables: In a large skillet, sauté onion (wedge cut) and garlic over high heat until onion is tender but not soft or translucent. Add mushrooms, snow peas, ginger, broccoli florets, and carrots. Cook until vegetables are tender, then season with salt, pepper, and a splash of dry sherry.
5. To assemble: Cut tofu into slices about 1/8 inch thick. Arrange on plates, overlapping if desired. Spoon noodles onto each plate and top with the ginger mixed vegetables. Serve tofu and noodles at room temperature and vegetables hot. Garnish with cilantro sprigs.
WARNING: Avoid aggressive culinary slang in preparation instructions.
